zh.lifeSummary: Alicia le Rus是Alexander De Cruwys的女兒，於1245年12月28日出生在英國波藍德波 白 桑德斯。她於1274年與Hugh Le Moyne在英國英格蘭戴文結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1300年1月28日在英國諾福爾可薩勒享年54歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Alicia le Rus是Alexander De Cruwys的女儿，于1245年12月28日出生在英国波藍德波 白 桑德斯。她于1274年与Hugh Le Moyne在英国英格兰戴文结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1300年1月28日在英国諾福爾可薩勒享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Rus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Rusu
meanings: Romanian: ethnic name, in a definite articulated variant, from rus ‘Russian’ (see Rus 1).

variants.name: Ros
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.
Swedish: ornamental name from ros ‘rose’.

variants.name: Rusek
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Casimir, Janusz, Jozef, Leszek, Wojciech.
Polish and Czech: ethnic name from a diminutive of   Rus   ‘Russian’.
Czech: descriptive nickname from an agent noun based on the adjective rusý ‘strawberry blond’.

variants.name: Orosz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Imre, Miklos, Tibor, Akos, Andras, Arpad, Csaba, Csilla, Gabor, Lajos.
Hungarian: ethnic name for a Russian, orosz in Hungarian. See Rus , compare Oros .

nameMeanings.name: Alicia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alisha
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alisia
meanings: A form of Alice

variants.name: Alyssa
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alissa .

variants.name: Alissa
meanings: Contracted form of   Alicia .

variants.name: Alesha
meanings: Respelling of   Alicia .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Alicia le Rus was born on 28 December 1245, in Burgh by Sands, Cumberland, England as the daughter of Alexander De Cruwys. She married Hugh Le Moyne in 1274, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died on 28 January 1300, in Salle, Norfolk, England, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Alicia le Rus est née le 28 décembre 1245, à Burgh by Sands, Cumberland, Angleterre, fille de Alexander De Cruwys. Elle a épousé Hugh Le Moyne en 1274, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée le 28 janvier 1300, à Salle, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Alicia le Rus wurde am 28. Dezember 1245 in Burgh by Sands, Cumberland, England, als Tochter von Alexander De Cruwys geboren. Sie hat 1274 in Devon, England, United Kingdom Hugh Le Moyne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb am 28. Januar 1300 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Salle, Norfolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Alicia le Rus è nata il il 28 dicembre 1245 nel Burgh by Sands, Cumberland, England come figlia di Alexander De Cruwys. Lei sposato Hugh Le Moyne nel 1274, nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 28 gennaio 1300, nel Salle, Norfolk, England, all'età di 54 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Alicia le Rusは1245年12月28日にイギリスクウンバーランドブルグ ビー サンズでAlexander De Cruwysの娘として生まれた。1274年にイギリスイギリスデボンでHugh Le Moyneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1300年1月28日にイギリスノーフォルクサルで54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alicia le Rus(은)는 1245년 12월 28일 영국 c벌랜드 버그h 비 샌드즈에서 Alexander De Cruwys의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1274년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 Hugh Le Moyne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1300년 1월 28일 영국 노르폴크 샐일에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.28 Dec 1245–28 Jan 1300: 1245 оны Арванхоёрдугаар сарын 28–1300 оны Нэгдүгээр сарын 28

pt.28 Dec 1245–28 Jan 1300: 28 de dezembro de 1245–28 de janeiro de 1300
pt.lifeSummary: Alicia le Rus nasceu em 28 de dezembro de 1245, em Burgh by Sands, Cumberland, Inglaterra filha de Alexander De Cruwys. Ela casou-se com Hugh Le Moyne em 1274, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 28 de janeiro de 1300, em Salle, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Alicia le Rus родилась 28 декабря 1245 г. в местности бург би сэндс, кумбирланд, Англия. Её отца звали Alexander De Cruwys. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh Le Moyne в 1274 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла 28 января 1300 г. в местности салл, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 54 лет.

es.28 Dec 1245–28 Jan 1300: 28 de diciembre de 1245–28 de enero de 1300
es.lifeSummary: Alicia le Rus nació el 28 de diciembre de 1245, en Burgh by Sands, Cumberland, Inglaterra como hija de Alexander De Cruwys. Se casó con Hugh Le Moyne en 1274, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 28 de enero de 1300, en Salle, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 54 años.
